primary_reason: The study decomposes benchmarked COVID-19 vaccination-survey error to diagnose how selection bias and the Big Data Paradox can produce precise but severely inaccurate uptake estimates.
The study combines population pharmacokinetic modeling with exposure simulations to derive age- and weight-specific abacavir dosing strategies for neonates exposed to or treated for HIV.

The study formulates and simulates a deterministic COVID-19 model that separates direct and environmental transmission and assesses sensitivity to alternative incidence functions and control parameters.

The study iteratively combines an agent-based biofilm model with experiments to distinguish possible competitive, surfactant, and growth-inhibition mechanisms between organisms involved in oral candidiasis.
The study uses metadynamics to compare antibiotic-candidate binding to a molecular riboswitch but contributes no epidemiological method.
The study simulates competing SARS-CoV-2 strains to assess how transmissibility, immune escape, epidemic size, and epidemic trajectory influence variant predominance.
The study develops a nursing-home agent-based transmission model calibrated to Austrian facilities and uses it to compare testing, vaccination, and visitor policies.
The study uses an agent-based university transmission model to compare timetabling, class-size, contact-frequency, testing, tracing, and vaccination scenarios for SARS-CoV-2 control.
The review summarizes the immunological mechanisms, safety, and vaccine-platform uses of emulsion adjuvants without developing, comparing, or prescribing an epidemiologic or surveillance method.

The agent-based model concerns the life cycle of pandemic-related online collective behavior rather than an infectious outcome, and the abstract provides no concrete route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
The study screens plant compounds against ACE2 using docking, toxicity prediction, and molecular dynamics rather than epidemiological inference.
The study develops an eight-state mechanistic epidemic model with recency-weighted parameter fitting, compares fitting and compartmental alternatives, and produces forecasts with uncertainty regions.
This cancer case report describes a pembrolizumab response in a malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor and supplies no infectious-disease outcome or concrete methodological transfer route.

The study develops and fits a mechanistic COVID-19 model containing airborne and direct-contact pathways and simulates vaccination, treatment, masking, and immigration controls in two Indian districts.
The simulation evaluates physicians' adherence to emergency management of status epilepticus, while respiratory infection is only background motivation rather than the modeled or measured outcome.
The study calibrates a school agent-based model with cluster-tracing data and simulates combinations of ventilation, class-size reduction, masking, vaccination, and entry testing.
The study applies an agent-based nursing-home model to quantify how community transmission, variants, staff vaccination, and resident boosters affect outbreak dynamics.
The study constructs an agent-based model of pre-vaccination COVID-19 spread, verifies it against Ukrainian surveillance statistics, and uses it to identify epidemic drivers.
The study develops modified red-blood-cell standards for electrical cytometry and separation, but infection is only a possible use context and no concrete infectious-disease transfer is demonstrated.
The study extends a viral-dynamics simulation framework to optimize rapid-antigen-test timing and consecutive-negative requirements for ending COVID-19 isolation.
The study evaluates a Bayesian hidden Markov surveillance model on simulated longitudinal herd infections, risk factors, and test results across varied epidemiologic and diagnostic scenarios.
The study models construction-project development and economic stability with fear of COVID-19 as a moderator, but it has no infectious outcome or concrete epidemiologic transfer route.
The study uses post-market surveillance data to estimate adverse-event incidence after vaccination and benchmarks those risks against several common medicine classes.
The retracted study synthesizes and screens antibacterial compounds with molecular docking but contributes no transferable epidemiological design.
The agent-based model concerns inheritance and mating-related prevalence of genetic deafness rather than infection, and the abstract identifies no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The study models within-host SARS-CoV-2 dynamics to simulate how variant reproduction rates and treatment timing alter the antiviral effectiveness of molnupiravir.
The benchmark compares tools for prioritizing human genetic-disease variants, and the abstract does not establish a concrete application to infectious pathogens or surveillance.
The study simulates proposed COVID-19 drug permeability through a cellular membrane without an epidemiological or population-infection method.
The study applies a demographic, spatial, and behavioral agent-based model to forecast SARS-CoV-2 policy scenarios and support reopening decisions in Australia and New Zealand.

The study derives causal estimands and an inverse-probability-weighted estimator for test-negative vaccine-effectiveness studies and uses simulation to compare it with logistic regression under effect modification and interference.
The study develops ensemble Kalman-filter strategies for calibrating epidemiologic agent-based models and evaluates joint state and parameter inference with synthetic and observed COVID-19 data.
The study predicts oncologic recurrence after surgery for HPV-positive cancer, but recurrence rather than infection is the outcome and no concrete infectious-epidemiology transfer route is supplied.
The record creates a molecular drug-screening benchmark across diseases but does not provide a concrete infectious-epidemiology transfer design.
The study develops an agent-based long-term-care outbreak tool, compares its predictions with observed outbreaks, and simulates testing, protective equipment, and combined controls.
The study uses simulated bacterial evolution as a gold standard to benchmark 19 complete outbreak-relevant phylogenomic workflows.
The study develops a high-performance, single-individual agent-based framework for simulating ten million people and evaluates its representation of COVID-19 spread and policies in Lombardy.
The study uses an agent-based transmission model to compare reactive vaccination around workplace and school cases with non-reactive rollout strategies under varied coverage and incidence.
The record is identified as a comment and provides no abstract or other evidence of substantive methodological, diagnostic, correction, or guidance content.
The study virtually screens plant protease inhibitors against a SARS-CoV-2 structural protein without a transferable epidemiological method.
The study derives and cross-validates risk-adjustment models for hospital quality benchmarking that include hospital-acquired urinary tract infection and pneumonia outcomes.

The study develops facility-specific agent-based models of Listeria cross-contamination, validates them against environmental sampling, and compares corrective cleaning and transmission-control actions.

The study benchmarks antibacterial susceptibility across pathogen strains and uses exact-match and clustering analyses to construct efficient nonredundant screening panels for candidate prioritization.
The study benchmarks mitochondrial variant callers and pathogenicity resources for human genetic disease without identifying a concrete route to infectious-disease analysis.
The study builds a spatially explicit agent-based model linking host dispersal, habitat fragmentation, tick demography, and pathogen transmission to heterogeneity in infected tick burdens.
The study fits a school agent-based transmission model to contact and screening data and performs a cost-benefit comparison of testing, vaccination, and closure protocols.
The study couples spatial movement and agent-based SIR models to compare indoor intervention strategies and relative transmission risks for several respiratory infections.
The study compares coronavirus receptor-binding structures by molecular dynamics to infer host adaptation without a population or epidemiological model.
The study computationally screens microbial compounds against two SARS-CoV-2 proteases for drug discovery rather than epidemiological inference.
The study uses an age-structured stochastic SEIR model informed by Greek contact data to compare second-dose-delay policies by infections, deaths, and life-years lost.

The study develops a weather- and host-phenology-driven mechanistic model of grapevine fungal infection and validates its predicted disease progression and infection periods against independent epidemics.
The study combines field observations with an agent-based pedestrian and airborne-transmission model to evaluate how fever-clinic capacity, patient flow, and layout affect cross-infection risk.
The agent-based model concerns melanoma tumor–immune interactions and cancer treatment, and the abstract provides no concrete route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
The study develops an agent-based SIR model with spatial risk indices to simulate how alternative mobility restrictions affect COVID-19 infections and deaths.
The study compares one compound against multiple SARS-CoV-2 protein targets using molecular docking and dynamics without epidemiological inference.
The airway-simulation study compares aerosol-mitigating intubation strategies, devices, and clinician groups using procedural success and timing outcomes relevant to infection control.
The paper develops and tests a spatiotemporal exposure model that combines fine-grained indoor trajectories, airborne decay, and layout-dependent transmission constraints.
The study implements a standardized electronic hand-hygiene observation system that provides immediate feedback and anonymized interhospital benchmarking.
A calibrated high-resolution agent-based model projects how waning immunity, booster rollout, and testing interact to shape COVID-19 resurgence.
The prospective chart review evaluates a longitudinal student-care model by benchmarking vaccination and screening performance against local clinician care and national estimates.
The paper develops a regularized infection-incidence deconvolution estimator, compares it with alternatives under simulated delay and censoring conditions, and supplies an R implementation.
The study adapts mechanistic zoonotic-transmission models to snake–human contact, providing a concrete transferable framework for spatially modeling environmentally mediated host interactions.
The paper proposes and demonstrates simulation-based methods for efficiently estimating and quantifying uncertainty in Bayesian adaptive-trial operating characteristics.
An agent-based nursing-home transmission model compares testing frequencies, vaccination priorities, and introduction risks using infection and hospitalization outcomes.
Through structured SIR simulations, the paper diagnoses why ordinary regression coefficients from dependent infectious-disease case data can be uninterpretable or spuriously significant.
The study introduces a transmission-chain estimator of epidemic case reporting and evaluates its bias and precision across simulated outbreak sizes and reporting levels.
The controlled mouse study comparatively benchmarks five vaccine adjuvants using durable antibody, cellular, innate-response, and variant-protection measurements.
The agent-based Monte Carlo study compares age-prioritized and random COVID-19 vaccination strategies in two countries with different population structures.
The study combines national administrative and blood-donor data with a Markov simulation to estimate current and future diagnosed, untreated, and treated hepatitis burdens.
The study uses a test-negative case-control design to estimate primary-series and booster effectiveness and waning against SARS-CoV-2 infection across variant periods.
Iterative in situ simulations identify workflow, equipment, role, and communication failures and use those findings to revise a COVID-19 resuscitation protocol.
The study screens phytochemicals against an HCV envelope protein using docking, pharmacokinetic prediction, and molecular dynamics rather than epidemiological inference.
A mobility-informed agent-based model estimates the timing, setting, and concentration of SARS-CoV-2 transmission and superspreading events in two metropolitan areas.
The record provides openly available reference-genome and simulated-metagenomic datasets designed for reproducible benchmarking of AMR-gene detection pipelines.
The study validates a spatially explicit agent-based influenza model using case data and applies it to optimize combinations of vaccination, masking, and quarantine.
The simulation study assesses whether reported age-specific vaccine efficacies are statistically plausible and identifies an exceptionally unlikely pattern in the Sputnik V results.
The paper develops and independently validates a dynamic weather-driven mechanistic model of fungal inoculum production, dispersal, infection, and disease progression across crops.
The study uses WHO benchmarking indicators and longitudinal self-assessment records to evaluate a capacity-strengthening model for emergency clinical-trial oversight in three countries.
The study describes patient, regional, and hospital variation in empiric antibiotic prescribing using conventional regression without identifying a reusable methodological contribution.
The study computationally designs a peptide-gold nanoparticle SARS-CoV-2 biosensor through molecular dynamics but supplies no empirical diagnostic evaluation or transferable epidemiological design.
The study benchmarks eight genome assemblers across multiple viral sequencing datasets and metrics and derives recommendations for viral genomic analyses.
The computational benchmarking study establishes reference interaction energies and compares lower-cost methods, including for an HIV–indinavir complex relevant to antiviral molecular modeling.
The paper develops and experimentally compares a large-scale epidemic simulator and reinforcement-learning framework for optimizing pandemic controls under health–economic trade-offs.

The study creates a labeled benchmark dataset and compares sentiment classifiers for measuring pandemic information flows, offering a concrete surveillance method for infectious-emergency communication and misinformation response.
The study combines outbreak records with SEIR and agent-based models to compare testing, vaccination, occupancy, and ventilation controls at different spatial scales.
A three-dimensional agent-based model represents Hong Kong's vertical urban environment to assess testing and lockdown interventions against an Omicron wave.
This review concerns agent-based modeling of community violence and mentions infectious-disease control only as an analogy without specifying a concrete transfer back to infectious-disease research.
The study constructs a configurable city-level agent-based digital twin to project COVID-19 transmission, healthcare demand, and intervention trade-offs.
The article prescribes performance-based, dynamically maintained datasets, metrics, validation procedures, and quality controls for benchmarking AMR bioinformatics pipelines.
The study applies incidence–prevalence and incidence–mortality benchmark metrics to modeled HIV estimates to measure and compare epidemic transitions across jurisdictions.
The molecular-dynamics analysis concerns cholesterol transport in Niemann–Pick disease, while infectious targeting is mentioned only incidentally.
A survey-informed multiagent mobility model simulates occupation-specific travel and validates spatial patterns used to locate potential malaria exposure.
The paper explains network-targeted maximum likelihood estimation and evaluates its causal-effect estimates across simulated interference settings, including infection transmission.
The record releases a curated, standardized, documented, and extensible open benchmark dataset for reproducible development of COVID-19 lung-ultrasound algorithms.
The paper diagnoses measurement-timing and selection-bias problems in historical HIV viral-load summaries, compares alternative summaries, and validates an imputation remedy by simulation.
The paper documents an animal-disease modeling challenge, its mechanistic multi-host epidemic generator, synthetic surveillance data, intervention scenarios, and lessons for future challenges.
A calibrated regional agent-based model uses open data to estimate COVID-19 vaccination impact and compare allocation strategies under alternative supply objectives.
Using empirical logger data and Monte Carlo simulations, the study compares how well alternative non-continuous vaccine-temperature monitoring practices detect storage excursions.
The study uses molecular dynamics to characterize conformations of a bacterial phosphatase for antibiotic discovery rather than epidemiological inference.
The paper develops an agent-based closed-environment transmission model for counterfactual evaluation of COVID-19 containment and reopening policies.
The multicenter study combines procedure-specific infection rates and volume estimates to create surgical-site-infection benchmarks and a burden-based prevention-prioritization framework.
This study uses a validated agent-based classroom transmission model with expert-informed contact networks to compare SARS-CoV-2 testing, quarantine, and contact-reduction strategies.
This study combines statistical estimates of variant transmissibility with a calibrated agent-based epidemic model to simulate intervention and vaccination scenarios in England.
This study combines a geospatial metapopulation agent-based model, renewal modeling, robustness analysis, and counterfactual simulations to estimate variant fitness and intervention effects.
This molecular docking and dynamics study screens a candidate SARS-CoV-2 compound but provides no transferable epidemiologic design, surveillance method, or population transmission model.
This registry-based simulation assigns catheter episodes to hypothetical scheduled-removal strategies to estimate their effects on bloodstream-infection incidence.
This study integrates agent-based migration and susceptible-infected-recovered transmission models to examine how habitat-network loss changes avian-influenza spread.
This study develops a neural ordinary-differential-equation method for generating multimodal synthetic patient trajectories and demonstrates it with infectious-disease epidemiological data.
This study applies stochastic queue-network simulation and stress testing to estimate COVID-19 vaccination-site capacity, waiting times, and resilience under alternative staffing assumptions.
This study constructs a clinical COVID-19 benchmark dataset and compares machine-learning detection performance with and without PCR results as a transferable diagnostic-measurement design.
Using HIV prevention-trial data, this study evaluates predicted serum neutralization titer as a surrogate endpoint and derives a quantitative efficacy benchmark for antibody regimens and vaccines.
This study builds and validates a GIS-based, one-to-one agent model of COVID-19 transmission for evaluating intervention strategies and testing burdens in Shanghai.
This study develops and verifies a mesoscale framework that couples agent interactions with respiratory-droplet flow physics for simulating indoor infection transmission.
This study validates a lifelong agent-based COVID-19 health-and-economic model and uses it to compare targeted vaccination and periodic-lockdown scenarios.
This molecular characterization of a mycobacterial protein is directed toward cancer research and supplies no concrete transfer route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
This study adapts an agent-based packinghouse contamination model to distinguish persistent from transient Listeria contamination and compare corrective sanitation strategies.
Using epidemics generated by an agent-based model, this study compares statistical estimators of vaccine-effectiveness waning, identifies bias, and proposes an improved time-partitioned Cox approach.
This bloodstream-infection cohort uses prespecified landmark analyses to avoid immortal-time bias when estimating the mortality consequences of delayed appropriate antimicrobial treatment.
This study fits an open-source agent-based model across 148 countries and uses counterfactual simulations to compare equitable and epidemiologically optimized COVID-19 vaccine allocations.
This study develops and compares two risk-adjustment models for benchmarking hospital-onset bacteremia and fungemia while accounting for facility characteristics and blood-culture testing practices.
This simulation study quantifies bias from nonrandom surveillance missingness and evaluates why weighting, larger samples, and conventional missingness tests fail to correct or detect it.
The supplied record identifies an agent-based COVID-19 epidemic simulation intended to assess regulatory measures and healthcare burden in Russia.
This study supplies standardized SARS-CoV-2 sequencing benchmark datasets, failure cases, mining and download scripts, and quality-control values for reproducible pipeline evaluation.
This study uses an age- and nutrition-stratified measles transmission model to simulate the joint effects of wasting treatment, nutritional supplementation, and vaccination.
This molecular docking, dynamics, and structure-activity study supports HBV compound design but does not contribute a transferable epidemiologic or population-level infectious-disease method.
This study develops a spatial agent-based Taenia solium model and a non-local calibration procedure validated in villages excluded from model fitting.
This study develops an open-source, spatially and temporally resolved agent-based framework for generating indoor contact networks and evaluating facility-specific outbreak policies.
The model predicts chemical respiratory sensitization, and its mention of COVID-19 comorbidity does not establish an infectious-epidemiology transfer route.
This study compares machine-learning surrogate and direct-posterior approaches for accelerating Bayesian inference in stochastic agent-based models, including an infectious-disease case study.
This study applies an LMIC-focused agent-based model to Zambia to compare symptomatic and asymptomatic rapid-testing allocations while explicitly representing non-COVID testing demand and isolation.
This study builds and experimentally validates a multiscale agent-based tissue model to investigate mechanisms and intervention targets for post-SARS-CoV-2 lung fibrosis.
This study uses a locally parameterized HIV agent-based model, scenario combinations, tree-based selection, and posterior prediction to identify prevention and care pathways for Chicago.
This survey benchmarks laboratory rodent husbandry practices for animal-welfare refinement without an infectious outcome or a concrete infectious-disease methodological transfer route.

This study benchmarks metagenomic taxonomic classifiers on simulated and real sequence data, characterizes species-specific misclassification, and provides a catalogue for detecting bioinformatic contamination.
This review synthesizes the role of tissue-resident memory T cells in mucosal infection control and recommends their measurement as a vaccine-immunogenicity benchmark.
This comparison of post-GWAS tools is conducted entirely in major depression and gives no concrete transfer route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
This simulation-based comparison quantifies how time-varying incomplete case detection biases cumulative counts, incidence, reproduction numbers, and doubling times during an epidemic.
This virtual screening and molecular-dynamics analysis prioritizes candidate SARS-CoV-2 inhibitors but offers no transferable epidemiologic design, surveillance method, or transmission model.
This study uses an agent-based trial emulation and randomization-based estimators to quantify modification of indirect HIV-prevention effects across sexual-network structures.
This study systematically benchmarks a new Nanopore sequencing configuration against an earlier configuration and next-generation sequencing for accurate SARS-CoV-2 genomic surveillance.
The study develops an open-source multi-host agent-based model to simulate environmental Leptospira transmission and compare control interventions.
Although it benchmarks two docking programs, this compound-screening study remains a molecular SARS-CoV-2 inhibitor analysis without a transferable epidemiologic or surveillance contribution.
This agent-based influenza study compares one- and two-dose vaccination schedules under varying epidemic timing, waning, uptake, and second-dose effectiveness.
This large longitudinal HIV cohort evaluates alternative viral-load strata as predictors of subsequent nonsuppression and failure and uses the results to propose a stricter monitoring benchmark.
This study develops a semi-mechanistic two-infected-cell-population model of long-term HBV markers and validates its prediction of combination-treatment response in a separate patient group.
This genetic and molecular-dynamics analysis concerns androgen insensitivity syndrome and provides no concrete transfer route to infectious-disease epidemiology.

This study benchmarks five HLA-typing algorithms across loci, sequencing depths, and clinically meaningful resolutions, directly supporting accurate HLA measurement in infectious-disease risk studies.
This Monte Carlo farm model integrates intramammary-infection transmission, milk production, culling, and treatment to estimate the economic burden of chronic infectious mastitis.
This study develops an intermediate-complexity mechanistic epidemic model with approximate Bayesian inference of time-varying transmission and evaluates its out-of-sample projection performance.
This molecular-dynamics investigation concerns amyotrophic lateral sclerosis protein dimerization and supplies no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The study optimizes an amikacin-binding aptamer for drug-derivative synthesis through binding experiments and molecular dynamics without a transferable infectious-disease epidemiological design.
